title: What really is Kaworus realization
wikitext:
[]: What, really, is Kaworu's realization?             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 1:53 am After re-watching Ep. 24, I thought about what exactly makes Kawrou want to die. He had already known that the Lillin were born from Lilith, but how did he know that the "white giant" in Terminal Dogma was Lilith, and why did this realization make him want to die, and for humanity to survive? And why would Seele send an Angel, Tabris, to initiate 3I? Wouldn't that be the opposite of what they want, and wouldn't it just destroy humanity? I can finally see some of the infamous plot holes emerging......

[]: Re: What, really, is Kawrou's realization?             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 2:29 am Well Kawrou appears to naturally know more about everything that is going on then most. He probably just naturally knew how Lilith and Adam looked. As for the realization, I think it breaks what we thought was a rule throughout the series and unveils the truth. We were told if an Adam-being met Adan himself, Third Impact would happen. This makes no sense if Lilim can met Lilith and still be fine. On an even deeper level, Lilim can engage in even more intimate things and still not cause the end of the world. The new rule which is "told" in episode twenty-four is that it's not Adam meeting his kids which ends the world. it's the unification of the Lilith and Adam lines. If Kawrou units with Lilith, then the forbidden unification would occur and cause Third Impact. The original theory was really just a plot to get NERV employees to actually help SEELE. As for wanting to die, he really wanted Shinji to be safe. Now rather or not Kawrou romantically loves Shinji is up to debate, but he still loves him at least on a friendship level and would prefer for him to survive. I'm not sure if he actually cared for the rest of the race. SEELE sent Kawrou to accelerate Third Impact before Gendo could start his version of it. They knew if Kawrou succeeded, Third Impact would occur under there scenario. The Lance would come back from the Moon and Unit-01, who is present at the event, would play her part as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 24, 2010 2:48 am You can try to wade through this thread which tries to figure out a lot of the random babble that happens in the 2nd half of episode 24.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Aug 24, 2010 5:01 am "Dum dee dum time to return to Adam. Wait, holy shit, this is Lilith! I know this since Adam used to be my own body so I would sort of know that. Wait, that means the Lilin...told me false information ON PURPOSE? Woah, I--woah. Between THAT, and the whole Evangelion thing...I was wondering why they would deliberately weaponize something they hated...do they REALLY want to live so badly that they'll literally do anything, no matter how awful? I didn't really care, personally. I was just going on instinct. Man, I guess it'd be way unfair for me to live and them to die. Darwin and whatnot." As for the realization, I think it breaks what we thought was a rule throughout the series and unveils the truth. We were told if an Adam-being met Adan himself, Third Impact would happen. This makes no sense if Lilim can met Lilith and still be fine. On an even deeper level, Lilim can engage in even more intimate things and still not cause the end of the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 3:30 pm AuraTwilight wrote: You're misunderstanding. It's not that there's a "rule" against a Seed and their children touching. It's because Adam's children want to merge with her and "resurrect" her so that they, as the new Adam, can basically finish what Second Impact started and destroy all life on the planet so the Angels can live there instead. Comparing to Lilith and the Lilim just isn't applicable.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Tue Aug 31, 2010 4:08 pm AuraTwilight wrote: "Dum dee dum time to return to Adam. Wait, holy shit, this is Lilith! I know this since Adam used to be my own body so I would sort of know that. Wait, that means the Lilin...told me false information ON PURPOSE? Woah, I--woah. Between THAT, and the whole Evangelion thing...I was wondering why they would deliberately weaponize something they hated...do they REALLY want to live so badly that they'll literally do anything, no matter how awful? I didn't really care, personally. I was just going on instinct. Man, I guess it'd be way unfair for me to live and them to die. Darwin and whatnot."

[]:             Quote Post by Agent_Koopa » Sat Sep 11, 2010 2:24 pm Well, even before Adam is delivered, Tokyo-3 is still built on top of the Black Moon, so maybe there's some lingering godly apparatus there they might wish to use?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Sep 11, 2010 3:03 pm Agent_Koopa wrote: Well, even before Adam is delivered, Tokyo-3 is still built on top of the Black Moon, so maybe there's some lingering godly apparatus there they might wish to use?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Sep 11, 2010 3:15 pm I just go with the Kaworu standard and say that the Angels mistake Evas and Lilith for Adam since they give off the same....*handwave* ...signature, or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Sep 11, 2010 4:29 pm Azathoth wrote: I can't find the quote for the life of me, but the CI says that some of the Angels were heading for Lilith, some were heading for Adam, some were heading for the Evas, and some were just rampaging around trying to fuck shit up. It's a rather weak answer, in my opinion; I would have preferred a more unified retcon.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Sep 11, 2010 4:46 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: The Angels improved a lot their actions from the "who are you guys? This is my backyard!" reaction of Sachiel to Arael and Armisael with their degrees in psychology; it's quite possible that not only their actions were independent but their purposes could have been different to some degree as well (like Kaworu in that early draft that I've read only recently who managed to figure out his purpose only after arriving at Neo Tokyo 3, but the same could be true for Evangelion 2's Kaworu as well, but I could be wrong about him).

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Sep 11, 2010 4:50 pm Finally, the discussion forum has a worthwhile topic. Now we just need something about Gendo's tanging, an actual explanation for the Bookend Reis, and an explanation for the first one assuming no concurrency.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Sep 11, 2010 5:09 pm Of course I do know that I'm not the first one to mention their various degree of knowledge, that's even mentioned in the series after Leliel's contact, it's just that in my opinion it's consistent with the fact that initially angels were simply going to Neo Tokyo 3 because they found it somehow inspiring and then they started elaborating more complex plans; for example Armisael's fusion with 00 (and the tempted fusion with 01) could indicate that he was going after the Evas more than after Lilith (or whatever he thought that was underground), he was simply floating around until an Eva appeared without tempting an attack; the same thing can be said for Arael, we don't have an idea about what he was planning to do after he finished to show Asuka that her life sucked a lot, his action is inconsistent with the actions of Angels that were merely pointing at Lilith (or Adam, depending on what they were thinking it was inside Terminal Dogma) like Ramiel or Zeruel; both could have simply realized somehow that without the removal of the Evas there was going to be an obstacle to their plan to merge with Adam/Lilith but we can't be sure about it (and for example Arael didn't even try to attack the 00 when he had the chance, even after the 02 was stopped and Rei was preparing herself to throw the spear). I just think that the CI in the regards of the Angels isn't that much of a retcon or at least it can be harmonized with the series quite well. The CI about the Angels aiming for Lilith (Special Agency Nerv D) that you've mentioned is not necessarily countered by Gaghiel's behaviour, it just explains as an high level info that the Angels are targeting that giant thing called Lilith and not Adam (an info that everyone have inside Nerv in the NME XD), but it doesn't say that they're targeting it exclusively, any being that resemble a FAR could have caused their attention.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Sep 12, 2010 2:27 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Finally, the discussion forum has a worthwhile topic. Now we just need something about Gendo's tanging, an actual explanation for the Bookend Reis, and an explanation for the first one assuming no concurrency.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Sep 12, 2010 5:58 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Threads on this already exist.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Sep 13, 2010 3:03 pm The questions were already answered in them to the satisfaction of most of the people who participated in them, then. But if you want to know more, just bump the threads.

[]: Re: What, really, is Kaworu's realization?             Quote Post by Sanada Yukimura » Sun Sep 26, 2010 12:10 am BeoX2 wrote: After re-watching Ep. 24, I thought about what exactly makes Kawrou want to die. He had already known that the Lillin were born from Lilith, but how did he know that the "white giant" in Terminal Dogma was Lilith, and why did this realization make him want to die, and for humanity to survive?

[]:             Quote Post by Da Games Elite » Tue Sep 28, 2010 7:44 pm Isn't it obvious? Kaworu saw Rei Ayanami and said "DAAAA~~AAAMNNN!!! I wanna get in that!"...so died and went into Gendo's hand...which went into Rei...yeah, the joke sucks, but whaddya gonna do? Anyway, all stupidity aside, I always figured Kaworu realized that Seele had manipulated him. Seeing as how Kaworu had been lead to believe Adam was inside Terminal Dogma, and that turned out to be oh-so-accurate, he probably started questioning everything he had ever been told, and probably died partially out of spite and partially in personal refusal to kill his best friend over something Kaworu didn't really want to happen (he didn't seem to want to merge with Lilith, just Adam...) Alternatively, he might've foreseen that Gendo had Adam in his hand, knew he'd use Rei for Instrumentality, and wanted to help Shinji find actualization...which would mean my joke at the beginning WASN'T too inaccurate...
